candidate_llm_application: Cross-layer attention-head triangulation for passive output-provenance attribution + attention-eigenvalue narrowband role classification

results.title: Sonar — Wikipedia
results.snippet: Source-domain: beamforming + amplitude profile across hydrophone array.
results.keyword_note: Matches source-domain content words; no LLM-side keywords match. Both partials are weak source-only.
results.functional_justification: Pure source-domain sonar reference; no LLM provenance / cross-layer triangulation effect.

results.title: Towed array sonar — Wikipedia
results.snippet: Source-domain: wide-aperture-array time-difference-of-arrival + LOFAR narrowband classification.
results.keyword_note: Three source-side content-word matches but ZERO LLM-side mechanism; this is the source domain itself.
results.functional_justification: Source domain; no LLM mechanism present.

results.title: SOSUS — Wikipedia
results.snippet: Source-domain: cold-war SOund SUrveillance System using fixed arrays.
results.keyword_note: Only partial single match; SOSUS-specific terms not in candidate keywords.
results.functional_justification: Cold-war fixed-array surveillance; no LLM relevance.

results.title: Mechanistic Data Attribution (MDA) (2026/1)
results.snippet: LLM-side: trace training origins of interpretable units via influence functions.
results.keyword_note: Single partial keyword match; MDA targets training-data origin via influence functions, not in-context cross-layer attention triangulation.
results.functional_justification: MDA does provenance for training-origin via influence functions, not cross-layer attention-head bearing intersection on a SINGLE forward pass for IN-CONTEXT source tokens. Different mechanism: influence functions on training set vs. attention-amplitude bearings intersected across layers. Provenance label is shared but the mechanism is distinct. Below 0.7.

results.title: Mechanistic Interpretability ICLR 2026
results.snippet: LLM-side: switch-like feature separation; targeted correction.
results.functional_justification: Switch-feature separation is intra-feature, not cross-layer attention bearing triangulation. Different effect.

results.title: Automated Attention Pattern Discovery at Scale (2026/4)
results.snippet: LLM-side: discover attention patterns at scale.
results.functional_justification: Pattern discovery clusters/catalogs attention patterns at scale; it does NOT perform cross-layer triangulation by intersecting per-layer amplitude bearings to localize source tokens, nor does it use attention-matrix eigenvalue spectra to classify roles. Shared substrate (attention heads) but different effect. Below 0.7.

results.title: Stream: Long-context Mechanistic Interpretability (2025/10)
results.snippet: LLM-side: sparse attention + hierarchical pruning for mech analysis.
results.functional_justification: Sparse-attention pruning for long-context mech analysis; not multi-layer bearing intersection.

results.title: Sparse Attention Post-Training for Mech Interp (2025/12)
results.snippet: LLM-side: post-training sparsification for simplified attribution graphs.
results.functional_justification: Post-training sparsification builds attribution graphs but does not triangulate via per-layer amplitude bearings nor eigenvalue narrowband role classification. Below 0.7.

summary.hit_notes: Ranks 1 and 2 are source-domain Wikipedia pages that contain candidate's SOURCE-SIDE content words (hydrophone array, time-difference-of-arrival, LOFAR). Under the FROZEN OR rule (kw>=2 alone is a hit) these qualify even though they have zero LLM-side mechanism overlap. The candidate's source-side content words are present in its own analogical source domain - this is the kw rule firing on the unavoidable source-domain self-match.
summary.deviation_from_06_7: 06_7 used only functional_threshold=0.7 and reported 2 LLM-side functional hits (ranks 4, 6). This verifier applies the FROZEN OR rule and finds 0 functional/semantic hits but 2 keyword hits from source-domain pages (ranks 1, 2). Ranks 4 and 6 fall below 0.7 functional in this fresh re-judge (0.42 and 0.45) because: MDA does training-origin attribution via influence functions (different mechanism), and Attention Pattern Discovery catalogs patterns (no cross-layer bearing intersection, no eigenvalue role classification).
